title: 10 best herbal teas for oily hair

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, irritation, and swelling associated with oily scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe tea neutralize free radicals, which can damage the hair follicles and contribute to oily hair issues, promoting healthy hair growth instead.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe tea's emollient compounds calm dryness and irritation on the scalp, helping to balance the natural oil production of the hair follicles and reduce excessive oiliness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance scalp pH and reduce oil production. The gel from the aloe plant contains salicylic acid, which gently exfoliates the scalp, removing dead skin cells and impurities that can clog pores and contribute to oily hair. By drinking aloe vera tea, you can promote a healthy scalp and reduce oiliness, leaving your hair looking cleaner and healthier for longer. It also helps to regulate hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Chromone: Chromone, specifically aloin, has been shown to reduce oil production in the scalp, thereby helping to control oily hair.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Aloe vera help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and preventing excessive oil buildup on the hair.
Saponins: Saponins in Aloe vera have astringent properties, which help to reduce oiliness and clean the scalp, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part for teas, as they contain a high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to balance oil production and reduce inflammation in the scalp.
Stems: Aloe vera stems are also used for teas, as they contain compounds like aloe-ester and aloe-glycoprotein that help to regulate oil production and promote a healthy scalp.

Barks: Although less commonly used, aloe vera bark is sometimes used for teas, as it contains aloe-verine, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help to soothe an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Wash your hands thoroughly before handling aloe vera gel to avoid contamination.
Cut an aloe vera leaf lengthwise to extract the gel inside with a spoon.
Mix 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Serve the aloe vera tea immediately and let it cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Sesame tea contains antioxidants like sesamin, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and contribute to oily hair, promoting a healthier scalp environment.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sesame tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, thereby reducing oil production and alleviating dandruff, itchiness, and irritation associated with oily hair.

Sesamin's ability to balance sebum production: Sesamin, a compound found in sesame tea, has been shown to regulate sebum production, helping to normalize oil levels in the scalp and reduce excessive oiliness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, teas helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds help to balance the scalp's pH levels, reduce oil production, and control sebum buildup. Sesame tea also has antifungal properties that prevent infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular consumption of sesame tea can help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production, leading to reduced oiliness and a healthier-looking mane. This natural remedy offers a chemical-free solution for oily hair.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: Sesamin, a lignan constituent, has been found to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in controlling the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp that contribute to oily hair.

Sesamolin: Sesamolin, another lignan constituent, is known to have antioxidant properties that help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, thereby controlling the production of sebum and reducing oily hair.

Fatty Acids (Specifically Oleic Acid): Sesamum indicum teas contain fatty acids, particularly oleic acid, which help in regulating the production of sebum in the scalp, thereby reducing oily hair and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are often used to make teas that help balance oil production and reduce greasiness due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used in teas to control sebum production, reduce dandruff, and soothe an itchy scalp.
Flowers: Sesame flowers are used in teas to help reduce excess oil and promote a healthy scalp due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 grams of sesamum indicum leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Dry the harvested sesamum indicum leaves and flowers in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Grind 1-2 grams of dried sesamum indicum leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground powder in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to serve warm or at room temperature immediately.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal teas like tea tree oil and peppermint contain antibacterial agents that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by clogging pores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Teas like chamomile and lavender have anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated scalps, reduce redness and itchiness, and calm inflammation caused by excessive oil production.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea and rosemary, help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair by weakening hair follicles and promoting sebum overproduction.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with oily hair because of its astringent properties. The catechins present in Camellia sinensis teas help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, the antioxidants in these teas help to regulate the hair growth cycle, preventing excessive oil secretion and promoting a balanced hair texture. This makes Camellia sinensis teas an effective natural remedy for controlling oily hair. Regular consumption can lead to a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These powerful phenolic compounds help reduce oil production in the scalp by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in oil secretion, thereby minimizing hair oiliness.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As potent antioxidants, flavonoids like Quercetin help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to oily hair.
Theaflavins: These unique flavanols formed during tea fermentation have been shown to possess anti-androgenic properties, which can help regulate sebum production in the scalp and reduce oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make tea that helps to balance oil production in the scalp and reduce dandruff.
Buds: Used to make tea that helps to regulate oil production and soothe an itchy scalp.
Stems: Not commonly used, but some teas may use Camellia sinensis stems to add antioxidants and help balance oil production in the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Select 5 grams of high-quality camellia sinensis leaves for a single serving of tea.
Rinse the camellia sinensis leaves in cold water for 10 seconds to remove impurities.
Boil fresh water in a teapot for 3-5 minutes or until it reaches a rolling boil.
Steep the camellia sinensis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for 2-3 minutes to reach a comfortable temperature.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass tea has antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tea can help reduce redness and swelling associated with oily scalps, promoting a healthier and more balanced scalp environment.
Astringent properties: Lemongrass tea has astringent properties that can help tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and helping to absorb excess sebum.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, teas helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to balance the scalp's natural oil production and reduce excess sebum. This, in turn, minimizes the appearance of greasiness and leaves hair looking healthier and more manageable. Regular consumption of lemongrass tea may also help to regulate the scalp's pH levels, promoting a balanced and healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene helps reduce sebum production, thereby controlling oily hair and scalp conditions.
Geraniol: As a natural astringent and antiseptic, geraniol helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Cymbopogon citratus for teas as they are rich in essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, which help in balancing oil production in the scalp.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used for teas as they contain a high concentration of citronella oil, which helps in reducing oil production and promoting a healthy scalp.
Roots: The roots are sometimes used for teas as they contain a mix of essential oils, including geraniol and limonene, which help in balancing oil production and reducing dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 bunches of the herb, cutting the stalks at a 45-degree angle to promote new growth.
Rinse the harvested herb in cold running water to remove any dirt or debris from the roots.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried herb or 2-3 sprigs of fresh herb in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or iced, garnished with a slice of citrus fruit and a sprig of the herb.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals in the scalp, which can contribute to an oily scalp and dandruff by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Stimulant properties: The stimulants present in rosemary tea help to increase blood flow to the scalp, which can help to reduce oil production and promote a healthy environment for hair growth by improving circulation and oxygenation of the scalp tissues.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Rosemary tea's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to combat scalp infections and irritations that can contribute to oily hair, such as fungal infections like ringworm or bacterial infections like folliculitis, by reducing the growth of microorganisms on the scalp.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, teas helps with oily hair because of its stimulating properties that improve blood circulation to the scalp. This increased flow of oxygen and nutrients helps to balance the oil production in the hair follicles, reducing excess oil and greasiness. Additionally, rosemary's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to control scalp infections and irritation, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing the likelihood of oily hair. Regular consumption of rosemary tea may also help to strengthen hair follicles.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene helps reduce oil production in the scalp by inhibiting the activity of sebum-producing enzymes, thereby controlling oily hair.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpene has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the scalp, reduce irritation, and promote a balanced oil production, leading to healthier and less oily hair.

Bornyl acetate: This monoterpenic compound has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help control scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment, reducing the likelihood of oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used to make tea for oily hair as they contain rosmarinic acid, which helps to balance scalp pH and reduce oil production.
Flowers: Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used in tea for oily hair as they have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the scalp.

Leaves: Buds: Buds: Rosmarinus officinalis buds are also used to make tea for oily hair as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce oil production and soothe the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a kettle to a rolling boil for 2 to 3 minutes.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 1 to 2 minutes before serving.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, yeast, and fungi on the scalp, reducing dandruff and other scalp irritations that can contribute to oily hair.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal properties of tea tree oil help combat fungal infections such as ringworm, which can cause excessive oil production and flakiness on the scalp, leading to oily hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an irritated scalp, reducing redness, itching, and inflammation that can exacerbate oily hair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, teas helps with oily hair because of its natural antiseptic and antifungal properties. The tea's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, work to balance the scalp's pH, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores. This results in a healthier scalp and reduced oiliness, leaving hair looking cleaner and fresher for longer. Regular use of tea tree tea may also help to purify the scalp, reducing dandruff and promoting a balanced hair growth cycle.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): This terpene helps reduce sebum production, which can contribute to oily hair.
Cymene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can lead to oily hair and dandruff.
Linalool: This terpene has a balancing effect on the scalp's oil production, helping to regulate sebum levels and reduce the appearance of oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help to balance oil production in the scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Buds: The buds contain antimicrobial properties that help to control oil and reduce inflammation in the scalp, leading to a healthier and more balanced hair.
Stems: The stems are used to extract essential oils, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to regulate oil production and prevent scalp irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried leaves from the melaleuca alternifolia plant with scissors.
Steep the dried leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes gently.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup immediately.
Add honey or other sweeteners to taste but no more than 1 teaspoon per cup.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving to your guests.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Antioxidants in Maidenhair Tree tea help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and scalp, reducing oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in the tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce inflammation on the scalp, which is often a contributing factor to oily hair.

Astringent properties: Maidenhair Tree tea has astringent properties that can help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and absorption, resulting in a reduction of excess oil and a healthier, more balanced scalp.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with oily hair because of its ability to balance scalp sebum production. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy hair growth. The tea's anti-oxidant properties also work to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing the production of excess oil that can lead to oily hair. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds can help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which may help to slow down the production of sebum, a major contributor to oily hair.

Bilobalide: A sesquiterpene trilactone found in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the occurrence of scalp conditions that contribute to oily hair.

Quercetin: A flavonoid found in Ginkgo biloba, quercetin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the production of sebum and promote a healthier scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Ginkgo biloba are often used to make teas for oily hair as they have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp, making them suitable for teas to combat oily hair.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are sometimes used in teas for oily hair due to their ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can help to regulate oil production.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves according to your desired strength.
Steep the measured leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired to sweeten the bitter flavor of the tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving and consuming the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Help to control bacteria on the scalp, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores that can contribute to oil production.
Antioxidant properties: Neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to hair follicles, promoting healthy growth and reducing oiliness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and swelling that can be caused by oily hair, while also helping to regulate sebum production.

remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps to reduce the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the hair follicles, thereby reducing the appearance of oily hair.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia for 1 teaspoon dosage at 7-8 am.
Steep the dried flowers in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes at 10 am.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth at 10:15 am.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours at 10:30 am.
Consume 1-2 cups of the herbal tea 2-3 times a day for optimal benefits at 11 am.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties help combat scalp infections and reduce dandruff, which can contribute to an oily scalp.

Steep 1-2 teaspoons of the harvested thymus vulgaris in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in the tea may help reduce redness and swelling associated with an oily scalp, promoting a healthier scalp environment and reducing dandruff and flaking.

remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves for tea making typically around 2 to 4 teaspoons.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 1 to 2 weeks until they become crumbly.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers or 1 tablespoon dried st john's wort.
Combine the harvested st john's wort with 1 quart boiling water in a large heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before use or immediately consume and enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with oily hair because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to an oily scalp. remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation that can contribute to oily hair.
Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The antimicrobial properties in ginger tea help to combat bacteria and fungi that can cause oil buildup and dandruff, leading to a healthier scalp and hair.

Detoxification and circulation improvement: Ginger's warming and stimulating properties help to improve blood flow and circulation to the scalp, promoting the removal of toxins and excess oils that can contribute to oily hair.

Barks: The barks of the ginger plant contain compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling scalp infections and reducing oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots or purchase dried ginger powder weighing approximately 10-15 grams for each serving.
Grind the dried ginger powder into fine particles using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Heat water to a temperature of 80-90 degrees celsius in a saucepan or kettle.
Add the ground ginger powder to the heated water in an amount of 1-2 teaspoons for every 250 milliliters.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes then strain and serve the tea hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with oily hair because its natural astringent and antiseptic properties help to balance the scalp's oil production, reducing excess sebum buildup. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers or dried herbs from reputable sources.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried herb or three teaspoons of fresh herb for each cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with one cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to dissolve and the flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids before consuming.

The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily hair, while its humectant properties lock in moisture, leaving hair feeling soft and manageable. Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow tea contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage hair and lead to oily buildup, promoting healthier and more balanced hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in marshmallow tea can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, calming irritation caused by oily hair and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse and release their properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before using it as a final rinse after shampooing.
Shampoo your hair as usual and then pour the cooled tea over your scalp and hair for 1-2 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with oily hair because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and astringent properties. The tea's natural oil-absorbing agents help to reduce excess sebum production, while its antifungal and antibacterial properties combat scalp infections that can exacerbate oily conditions. Additionally, the tea's ability to balance pH levels in the scalp helps to regulate the natural oil production process, leaving hair looking healthy, manageable, and less greasy. Regular consumption of purple coneflower teas can lead to a significant reduction in oiliness and an improvement in overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower tea contains compounds like echinacein and cichoric acid, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with oily hair.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial activity helps to combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Astringent and antiseborrheic properties: Purple coneflower tea has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like acne and eczema, and its astringent and antiseborrheic effects can help tighten pores, reduce oil production, and soothe irritated scalps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting healthy hair growth.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, buds have natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to control sebum production and prevent acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for your desired dosage.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the harvested herbs with 1 cup boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Use 1/2 cup of the cooled echinacea purpurea tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help balance scalp ph.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The tea's tannins also help to tighten and refine pores, reducing excess sebum from clogging the hair follicles. Additionally, stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory effects soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking that can contribute to an oily hair condition. Regular consumption of stinging nettle teas can lead to a healthier, more balanced scalp, resulting in manageable and stylishly shiny hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tea helps to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff by soothing redness and irritation.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle tea is rich in antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals in the scalp, which can damage hair follicles and lead to oily hair and other scalp issues.
Astringent properties: Stinging nettle tea has astringent compounds that help to constrict pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and controlling sebum buildup that contributes to oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, which helps to absorb excess oil and purify the scalp.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh stinging nettle leaves or one teaspoon dried leaves from the urtica dioica plant.
Rinse the fresh leaves with clean water and pat dry with paper towels before using them.
Steep one teaspoon of dried urtica dioica leaves or one tablespoon of fresh leaves in one cup boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea solution through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl or teapot, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before using it as a final rinse after shampooing and conditioning hair, letting it sit for 1-2 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with oily hair because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production. The tea's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation and soothes an itchy scalp, allowing for a healthier balance of sebum. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to strengthen the hair follicles, reducing the appearance of greasiness and promoting healthy hair growth. As a result, regular consumption of turmeric tea can lead to a reduction in oil production and a more balanced, manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric's curcuminoids have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce scalp inflammation that contributes to oily hair and dandruff.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage hair follicles, leading to oily hair and scalp issues. Antioxidants also promote healthy hair growth and strengthen hair roots.
Natural antibacterial agent: Turmeric's curcuminoids have antibacterial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which contributes to oil production and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in curcuminoids, which help to reduce sebum production and regulate oil secretion in the scalp, making them an effective ingredient for balancing oily hair.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the turmeric plant is a key source of curcumin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe irritated scalps and reduce dandruff associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder curcuma longa.
Heat one cup of water to boiling point for five minutes duration.
Steep the measured turmeric powder in heated water for ten minutes soaking time.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature for thirty minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose teas helps with oily hair because it contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that works to balance the scalp's natural oil production. This can help reduce excess sebum, leaving hair feeling clean and refreshed without stripping it of its natural moisture. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe irritated scalps and promote healthy hair growth. As a result, evening primrose teas can provide a gentle and nourishing solution for those struggling with oily locks.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): Helps to reduce inflammation and regulate sebum production, leading to a balanced scalp pH and manageable oil levels.
Antioxidants: Neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and lead to excessive oil secretion, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Soothe the scalp and reduce redness and irritation caused by oily hair, allowing for improved blood circulation and healthier hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce dandruff.
Seeds: High in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to regulate sebum production and soothe irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried oenothera biennis flowers and measure out one tablespoon of the herb for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured oenothera biennis flowers with boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant pitcher and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl to remove the herb residue and discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of dried lavender flowers and one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the oenothera biennis tea mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the prepared herbal tea into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, allowing it to chill before use as a final rinse after shampooing oily hair.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal teas are acne, dandruff, and eczema. Certain herbs like chamomile, calendula, and tea tree extract have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with these conditions. By incorporating herbal teas into one's routine, individuals can achieve a balanced and healthy scalp and skin, alleviating symptoms of oily hair-related issues.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily hair are a natural remedy that uses the antioxidants and antifungal properties of herbs to balance the scalp's oil production and reduce greasiness. Examples of beneficial herbal teas include peppermint, sage, and rosemary, which help to purify the scalp and stimulate blood flow. By incorporating these teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can experience reduced oiliness, improved scalp health, and a fresher, cleaner feel, ultimately enhancing their overall confidence and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with oily hair is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and rosemary. Peppermint tea helps to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production, while chamomile tea soothes the scalp and reduces inflammation. Rosemary tea, rich in antioxidants, promotes healthy hair growth and improves circulation to the scalp. By steeping these teas in hot water, then applying the cooled liquid to the hair and scalp, you can achieve a healthy, balanced, and oil-free mane.
